abstract: The evolving fifth generation (5G) of cellular wireless networks is envisioned to deliver a transformative leap in performance, characterized by significantly higher data rates, improved end-user quality-of-experience (QoE), ultra-low latency, and reduced energy consumption. This article provides a comprehensive overview of the emerging technologies poised to define the 5G landscape, including network densification, full-duplex communication, massive MIMO, and cloud-based radio access networks (C-RAN). Beyond the architectural shifts, we explore the critical research hurdles these technologies introduce—specifically focusing on the complexities of radio resource management. Finally, we highlight the pressing measurement and test challenges essential for validating 5G system components and ensuring their real-world viability.
